title: Statement by Governor Montgomery on New COVID Restrictions
summary: Governor Montgomery addresses public concerns on COVID measures.
body: My Fellow Washingtonians, I want to speak to you directly and honestly about the challenge now facing our state.

Some of you have raised concerns that our response to COVID-19 has been too aggressive and that we are acting with more urgency than the current situation seems to demand. I hear you. And I want to explain, clearly and without reservation, why we are choosing to act now rather than wait.

I am a physician. Medicine has been my life's work. We are fortunate to live in an era of unprecedented medical advancement. Yet, even with every tool modern science has given us, history has shown us time and again that a respiratory virus, left unchecked, does not wait for us to catch up. It moves faster than our hospitals can respond, faster than our supply chains can adapt, and faster than our communities can absorb. By the time it looks serious, it often already is.
That is precisely why we are acting today.

Effective immediately, the state government will begin distributing N95 masks, hand sanitizer, and hand soap to residents through government-operated facilities, in addition to the resources and guidelines announced earlier today. These are the frontline tools of prevention, and getting them into your hands now is one of the most consequential things we can do.

But the state cannot do this alone. What happens next depends, in large part, on each of you. Wash your hands. Wear your mask in crowded spaces. Stay home if you feel unwell. These actions are small, but their collective impact is enormous.

My responsibility to you is your safety. I will not compromise on that, and I will not apologize for getting ahead of this. I would always rather be accused of doing too much too soon than too little too late.
We will face this together, with clear eyes, steady hands, and a shared commitment to one another. I place high value on the trust that our state places in itself. I ask now that you all do the same.
Thank you.

title: Statement On The COVID-19 Pandemic
summary: Statement from the office of Governor Montgomery on the looming threat that the coronavirus is bringing, and the steps being taken to ameliorate it in Washington.
body: As we are all aware, a novel respiratory virus known as COVID-19 has begun spreading internationally and has now been identified in the United States, including on the West Coast. The State of Washington must take immediate and comprehensive action to mitigate transmission and protect public health

I have spent my entire life in medicine. In the recently enacted state budget, we appropriated over 76 billion dollars for the Washington Department of Health and Human Services over the next fiscal year. Seventeen billion of that was for public health preparedness, R&D, and risk mitigation.

I have just recently signed an order accessing those funds for the purpose of managing the coronavirus pandemic. We are pledging a five billion dollar research donation stipend to assist in developing a vaccine for the coronavirus, in collaboration with university research departments and the federal government, as well as the rest of the money being redirected to Washington's emergency management and healthcare authorities to assist in stopping this before it starts.

In accordance with public health guidance, the State of Washington is implementing mandatory social distancing requirements for public and private facilities, including a minimum six-foot spacing standard.

The order also declares a state of emergency and the necessity of a partial lockdown mandate, wherein a curfew has been established in the State of Washington, and it is strongly recommended that Washingtonians remain out of public as much as possible. Certain public facilities will also no longer be publicly accessible for the duration of the emergency, including schools, where we are working on migration to a virtual education model for Washington public school students.
We are doing everything we can to keep Washingtonians safe from this looming crisis. We ask that you all play your part in helping keep yourselves, your neighbors, and your families safe.

title: Governor Montgomery Announces Death Penalty Ban Referendum
summary: Governor Addison Montgomery has declared a ballot referendum for the end of the death penalty in Washington State in the 2020 elections.
body: It is my responsibility as Governor of this state to protect the citizens therein and utilize the tools at my disposal in a benevolent manner consistent with the high-trust society we have built for ourselves since our admission to the union in 1889.

I have a responsibility to the citizens of Washington to support and ensure their rights to life and liberty. It is for that reason simply that the state should have no bearing on who lives and who dies, especially through an often unjust criminal "justice" system.

As such, I am proud to sign the bill presented by the state legislature to allow for a ballot referendum in the 2020 general election on the addition of an amendment to the Washington Constitution to ban the death penalty and scrub provisions allowing for capital punishment from state law.

It is the responsibility of no government to claim the lives of its citizens, no matter how heinous their actions. Capital punishment is a lazy and unfortunate means of refusing to deliver proper justice.

title: Governor Montgomery Declares Right To Choose Referendum
summary: Governor Addison Montgomery declares a constitutional referendum for the 111th amendment, the Right To Choose Amendment.
body: The fundamental healthcare privileges of women across Washington are currently stranded in the open air. As an OB/GYN, I would be remiss not to stand up for a woman's right to decide for herself what she does with her own body.
As such, in the 2020 election, a ballot referendum will be held to determine whether Washington should add the following language to the state constitution as the 111th amendment:

"The right of a citizen of the state of Washington to decide whether they may undergo the termination of a pregnancy shall not be infringed by the State of Washington at any period preceding 24 weeks of gestation for any reason, and may not be infringed thereafter should the citizen's life be at risk if the pregnancy were not to be terminated."
